mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 5.0%, or interaction term IC < 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which translates into positive short-term equity returns as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during periods of rapid inflation, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 4.0% while the federal funds rate remains below 3.0%, negative real yields erode purchasing power and force delayed monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns as markets price in aggressive future rate hikes.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for term premium.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive payroll growth coincides with negative equity returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0% quarter-over-quarter signals economic contraction and weakening aggregate demand, prompting equity market sell-offs and negative forward S&P 500 returns within the following month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when accompanied by expansionary fiscal policy, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and contraction in the unemployment rate confirms broad-based economic strength, generating immediate positive risk sentiment and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >50% of instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance when GDP growth is negative.
